---
title: Create Service
description: Set up and deploy your first Restate service with functions
---

Create a Restate service by defining functions that handle requests. Under the hood, Restate marks each function you export as a handler—i.e. the code that actually executes when a request comes in.

## Choose your service type

Start by deciding which type of service fits your use case:

**Service**: For stateless functions that process requests without storing data between calls. Perfect for data processing, API integrations, and business logic.

**Virtual Object**: For stateful functions that need to store and manage data per unique key. Perfect for user accounts, shopping carts, or any entity that needs persistent state.

**Workflow**: For long-running functions that coordinate complex processes over extended periods. Perfect for approval workflows, multi-step transactions, or orchestration scenarios.

## Set up your project

<CodeGroup>
```bash TypeScript
npx @restatedev/create-app@latest --template hello-world-http my-service
cd my-service
npm install
```

```bash Java
curl -LO https://github.com/restatedev/sdk-java/releases/latest/download/restate-sdk-java-starter.zip
unzip restate-sdk-java-starter.zip -d my-service && cd my-service
./gradlew build
```

```bash Python
pip install restate-sdk
mkdir my-service && cd my-service
```

```bash Go
go mod init my-service
go get github.com/restatedev/sdk-go
```
</CodeGroup>

## Define your functions

Create functions based on your chosen service type:

<CodeGroup>
```typescript TypeScript - Service
import { endpoint, service } from "@restatedev/restate-sdk";

const myService = service({
  name: "MyService",
  handlers: {
    // Define a function
    processData: async (ctx, input: { data: string }) => {
      // Process the input
      const result = await ctx.run("transform", () => transformData(input.data));
      
      return { processed: true, result };
    },
    
    // Define another function
    validateInput: async (ctx, input: { value: string }) => {
      const isValid = await ctx.run("validate", () => validate(input.value));
      
      return { valid: isValid };
    }
  }
});

// Start the HTTP endpoint
endpoint().bind(myService).listen();
```

```typescript TypeScript - Virtual Object
import { endpoint, object } from "@restatedev/restate-sdk";

const userAccount = object({
  name: "UserAccount",
  handlers: {
    // Function to update user profile
    updateProfile: async (ctx, profile: UserProfile) => {
      ctx.set("profile", profile);
      ctx.set("lastUpdated", new Date().toISOString());
      
      return { success: true, profile };
    },
    
    // Function to get user profile
    getProfile: async (ctx) => {
      const profile = await ctx.get<UserProfile>("profile");
      
      return { profile };
    }
  }
});

endpoint().bind(userAccount).listen();
```

```typescript TypeScript - Workflow
import { endpoint, workflow } from "@restatedev/restate-sdk";

const orderWorkflow = workflow({
  name: "OrderWorkflow",
  handlers: {
    // Main workflow function
    processOrder: async (ctx, order: Order) => {
      // Step 1: Validate order
      const validation = await ctx.run("validate", () => validateOrder(order));
      if (!validation.valid) {
        throw new Error("Invalid order");
      }
      
      // Step 2: Wait for payment confirmation
      const paymentConfirmed = await ctx.promise<boolean>("payment-confirmed");
      if (!paymentConfirmed) {
        return { status: "cancelled" };
      }
      
      // Step 3: Process fulfillment
      await ctx.sleep(1000); // Wait 1 second
      const fulfillment = await ctx.run("fulfill", () => fulfillOrder(order));
      
      return { status: "completed", fulfillmentId: fulfillment.id };
    },
    
    // Signal function to confirm payment
    confirmPayment: async (ctx, confirmed: boolean) => {
      ctx.promise("payment-confirmed").resolve(confirmed);
    },
    
    // Query function to check status
    getStatus: async (ctx) => {
      const status = await ctx.get<string>("status");
      return { status: status ?? "processing" };
    }
  }
});

endpoint().bind(orderWorkflow).listen();
```

```java Java - Service
import dev.restate.sdk.annotation.Handler;
import dev.restate.sdk.annotation.Service;
import dev.restate.sdk.http.vertx.RestateHttpEndpointBuilder;

@Service
public class MyService {
    
    // Define a function
    @Handler
    public ProcessResult processData(Context ctx, DataInput input) {
        // Process the input
        String result = ctx.run("transform", () -> transformData(input.getData()));
        
        return new ProcessResult(true, result);
    }
    
    // Define another function
    @Handler
    public ValidationResult validateInput(Context ctx, ValidationInput input) {
        boolean isValid = ctx.run("validate", () -> validate(input.getValue()));
        
        return new ValidationResult(isValid);
    }
}

// Start the HTTP endpoint
public static void main(String[] args) {
    RestateHttpEndpointBuilder.builder()
        .bind(new MyService())
        .buildAndListen();
}
```

```python Python - Service
from restate import Service, Endpoint

# Create service
my_service = Service("MyService")

# Define a function
@my_service.handler()
async def process_data(ctx, input: dict):
    # Process the input
    result = await ctx.run("transform", lambda: transform_data(input["data"]))
    
    return {"processed": True, "result": result}

# Define another function  
@my_service.handler()
async def validate_input(ctx, input: dict):
    is_valid = await ctx.run("validate", lambda: validate(input["value"]))
    
    return {"valid": is_valid}

# Start the HTTP endpoint
if __name__ == "__main__":
    endpoint = Endpoint()
    endpoint.bind(my_service)
    endpoint.listen()
```
</CodeGroup>

## Start your service

Run your service locally to test it:

<CodeGroup>
```bash TypeScript
npm run dev
```

```bash Java
./gradlew run
```

```bash Python
python app.py
```

```bash Go
go run main.go
```
</CodeGroup>

Your service will start on `http://localhost:9080` (or the port you configured).

## Register with Restate

Before you can call your functions, register your service with Restate:

```bash
# Register your service (run this once)
restate deployment register http://localhost:9080

# Check registered services
restate services list
```

Restate discovers all your functions automatically and makes them available for invocation.

## Test your functions

Once registered, you can call your functions:

<CodeGroup>
```bash cURL - Service
curl localhost:8080/MyService/processData \
  --json '{"data": "hello world"}'
```

```bash cURL - Virtual Object
curl localhost:8080/UserAccount/user123/updateProfile \
  --json '{"name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com"}'

curl localhost:8080/UserAccount/user123/getProfile
```

```bash cURL - Workflow
curl localhost:8080/OrderWorkflow/order456/processOrder \
  --json '{"customerId": "user123", "items": [{"id": "item1", "quantity": 2}]}'

# Send signal to workflow
curl localhost:8080/OrderWorkflow/order456/confirmPayment \
  --json 'true'

# Query workflow status
curl localhost:8080/OrderWorkflow/order456/getStatus
```
</CodeGroup>

## Next steps

Now that you have a working service:

1. **Add more functions** to handle different aspects of your business logic
2. **Call other services** using request-response, one-way, or delayed calls
3. **Handle external systems** safely with `ctx.run()` patterns
4. **Deploy to production** using your preferred deployment method

Your functions automatically get durability, retries, and exactly-once execution guarantees - no additional configuration needed.
